title: How to use Claude: a beginner's guide for 2026
lede: Claude is an AI assistant that's especially good at writing, long documents, and code. To use it well, give it context, a role, and clear instructions — then iterate. Here's how to start and what it's best for.
keyTakeaways: Claude (by Anthropic) is an AI assistant best for writing, careful reasoning, long documents, and code.
It's free to start at claude.ai — type a request in plain language and refine the answer.
Claude follows nuanced, multi-part instructions closely, so detailed prompts pay off.
Like every AI tool, it can be confidently wrong — verify anything important.
description: How to use Claude in 2026: a beginner's guide to Anthropic's AI — how to start, what it's best at, how to prompt it well, and how it compares to ChatGPT.

sections.heading: How do you use Claude?
sections.html: To use Claude, go to claude.ai (or the app), type what you want in plain language, and refine the answer with follow-ups. Claude is an AI assistant made by Anthropic, and it's especially strong at writing, careful reasoning, working through long documents, and code. It's free to start. The better your prompt — context, a role, clear instructions, and the format you want — the better Claude's output.

sections.heading: Getting started with Claude
sections.html:
1. Go to claude.ai or download the app and sign up — the free tier is enough to learn on.
  2. Type a real request, e.g. "You are an editor. Tighten this paragraph and flag anything unclear: [paste]."
  3. Iterate — "make it warmer," "shorter," "add an example" — until it's right.

No technical skills required — see learning AI without coding.

sections.heading: What is Claude best at?
sections.html:
• Writing — natural prose and editing that needs nuance.
  • Long documents — summarizing and reasoning over big inputs.
  • Code — explaining, writing, and reviewing it.
  • Following detailed instructions — it handles multi-part requests carefully.

Dig deeper at learn Claude.

sections.heading: How to prompt Claude well
sections.html: The same recipe that works everywhere works with Claude: give context, assign a role, state the task, and specify the format. Claude rewards detail — it follows nuanced instructions closely, so spelling out exactly what you want (and what to avoid) gets sharp results. Full walkthrough: how to write a good AI prompt. And as with any model, verify important claims.

sections.heading: Claude vs ChatGPT: which should you use?
sections.html: Use Claude when text quality matters most — long writing, careful reasoning, code. Use ChatGPT as the more versatile all-rounder with the biggest ecosystem. Many people keep both. The full breakdown is in ChatGPT vs Claude. Whichever you pick, the skill of directing it — AI fluency — matters more than the choice. Check yours with the free AI IQ test.

faq.q: How do I start using Claude?
faq.a: Go to claude.ai or the Claude app, sign up (the free tier is enough), and type a request in plain language. Refine the answer with follow-ups like 'make it shorter' or 'more formal.' No technical knowledge required.
Yes, Claude has a capable free tier that's plenty for learning and everyday tasks. Paid plans add higher limits and more advanced models.
Claude is especially good at writing, careful reasoning, working through long documents, and code. It follows detailed, multi-part instructions closely, so it rewards specific prompts.
Claude is often preferred for writing and code; ChatGPT is the more versatile all-rounder. Neither is universally better — it depends on your main task, and many people use both.

faq.q: How do I get good at Claude quickly?
faq.a: Use it daily on real work and practice writing clear, constrained prompts. A few minutes a day compounds fast — here is how to learn AI in 5 minutes a day and build overall AI fluency, not just one tool.
